mnemonics: B.C.A.T.S. BITES: Remember the main free space management techniques: Bitmap, Contiguous Allocation, Linked Lists, Indexed Blocks, and the Trade-offs for Space and Speed.
keyPoints: A bitmap uses a series of bits to represent the status (free/allocated) of each disk block.
Linked allocation stores pointers to the next free block, eliminating external fragmentation but increasing seek time.
The goal of free space management is to minimize fragmentation and efficiently service file allocation requests.
commonMistakes: Confusing internal fragmentation (unused space within an allocated block) with external fragmentation (free space is broken into small, unusable pieces).
Assuming that contiguous allocation is the best method because it offers fast access, while ignoring its severe external fragmentation problem.
